data.title: Formula Systems GAAP EPS of $1.28, revenue of $691.5M
data.description:
• Formula Systems press release (NASDAQ:FORTY): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.28.
  • Revenue of $691.5M (+8.0% Y/Y).

data.title: Affirm resumed at BMO with Outperform rating on expectation of GMV/revenue upside
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets resumed coverage of buy now, pay later lender Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) with an Outperform rating and a $69 price target, implying a 45% price increase in the next year.

Affirm's stock has slid by more than 40% from recent highs as the probability of a recession increases and investors digest the expected impact from Klarna's OnePay deal to provide installment loans for Walmart customers.

"We expect gross merchandise value/revenue less transaction costs ("RLTC") to surprise positively (despite modest impact from Klarna-OnePay); potential credit headwinds already anticipated," analyst Rufus Hone wrote in a note to clients.

BMO's forecasts assume a mini credit cycle in FY2026, but foresees a path to ~$4.00 adjusted EPS by FY2027 in a normalized credit environment.

Affirm's (NASDAQ:AFRM) partnership with Apple (AAPL) Pay could drive low single-digit billions of incremental GMV for AFRM as early as FY2026, Hone noted.

"We expect AFRM to benefit from medium-term GMV/RLTC upside; expectations appear to contemplate a steep deceleration, despite accelerating D2C (direct-to-consumer) growth, strong momentum supporting Affirm at Checkout, and RLTC tailwinds from lower interest rates,"  the analyst said.

Furthermore, BNPL is experiencing global secular growth, driven by demographic tailwinds; i.e., younger consumers prefer BNPL financing.

BMO's Outperform rating on Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating, both at Hold, and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

Affirm (AFRM) stock rose 0.8% in Thursday afternoon trading.

data.title: Caleress projects $2.80-$3.20 EPS for 2025 amid conservative outlook and tariff impacts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Caleres (CAL) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jay Schmidt highlighted that fourth-quarter earnings were at the high end of guidance, supported by market share gains in women's fashion footwear. Key brands, including Sam Edelman, Allen Edmonds, Naturalizer, and Vionic, outperformed. Growth in sneaker penetration and strategic investments were made to support long-term growth, while expenses were reduced in other areas.

  • New supply chain strategies were emphasized, with expectations that 75% of direct product sourcing will be outside of China by Q2 2025, aiming to mitigate the impact of additional tariffs.

  • CFO Jack Calandra noted adjusted earnings per share for Q4 2024 of $0.33 and full-year adjusted earnings per share of $3.30. Gross margin for Q4 was 43%, a decline from the previous year.

Outlook

  • Management provided guidance for 2025, projecting full-year consolidated sales to range from a decline of 1% to growth of 1%, with Famous Footwear sales expected to decline low single digits and Brand Portfolio sales growing 1%-2%.

  • Earnings per diluted share for 2025 are projected between $2.80 and $3.20, incorporating assumptions about tariff impacts.

  • Q1 2025 is expected to see a sales decline of 5%-6% and earnings per diluted share between $0.35 and $0.40.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter consolidated sales were $639.2 million, down 8.3%, including an unfavorable impact of $30.3 million related to the 53rd week in 2023.

  • Brand Portfolio sales declined 7.2%, with softness in boots and value-oriented brands, though contemporary brands like Allen Edmonds and Vionic showed strength. Famous Footwear's comparable sales declined 2.9%, with performance varying across categories.

  • Inventory ended the year at $565 million, up 4.5%, with efforts to shift product sourcing outside China and reduce aged inventory.

Q&A

  • Ashley Owens, KeyBanc Capital Markets, asked about the EPS guidance and quarterly improvement expectations. CFO Jack Calandra explained sequential improvements are expected due to new product introductions and leadership impact, along with benefits from international growth and new initiatives like Favorite Daughter.

  • Laura Champine, Loop Capital, inquired about brand portfolio sales trends. Management confirmed improvement is expected from specific initiatives, even with some decline in base business performance.

  • Mitch Kummetz, Seaport Research, questioned the gross margin impact of tariffs. Management estimated a 30-40 basis point gross margin impact due to absorbing tariff costs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over challenges in boots sales and tariff impacts but were optimistic about sequential improvements and new brand initiatives. Management's tone remained cautiously optimistic, emphasizing strategic investments and confidence in long-term growth.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated a more measured tone, with a focus on addressing headwinds while highlighting progress in strategic areas.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 reflects a more conservative outlook compared to the previous quarter, factoring in continued economic uncertainty and tariff impacts.

  • Management's tone shifted toward caution, addressing current challenges while maintaining confidence in long-term strategies.

  • Analysts' focus shifted more toward concerns about tariff impacts and gross margin trends than in the prior quarter.

  • Key metrics showed a decline, with sales down 8.3% year-over-year in Q4 compared to a 2.8% decline in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Additional tariffs on imports from China are expected to impact gross margins, with mitigation strategies including factory negotiations, selective price increases, and shifts in sourcing.

  • Softness in boots and value-oriented brands remains a concern, potentially impacting short-term sales trends.

  • Economic uncertainty and cautious consumer spending present ongoing challenges.

Final Takeaway

Caleres delivered Q4 earnings at the high end of its guidance but faces headwinds from tariffs and softness in key categories like boots. Management remains focused on mitigating these challenges through sourcing adjustments, strategic investments, and product innovation. Guidance for 2025 reflects a cautious approach, with projected EPS of $2.80-$3.20 and efforts to drive sequential improvements throughout the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lyft plans AV launch as soon as this summer
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) is moving forward with its plan to introduce autonomous vehicles to its driver fleet as recently signed agreements with May Mobility, Mobileye (MBLY), and Marubeni (OTCPK:MARUF) (OTCPK:MARUY) will enable a rollout on the platform this summer.

To assuage drivers’ worries that the increased availability of AVs will eat into earnings, Lyft’s EVP of Driver Experience, Jeremy Bird, assures drivers that even though AVs will eventually account for a larger share of cars, and a larger percentage of rides, ride-share is not a “zero-sum game.”

“Adding cars to our platform — whether they’re driven by humans or AVs — improves arrival times and creates a better rider experience. That gets people to take more rides, creating more opportunities for drivers. It’s a virtuous circle, and it’s as close to an economic law as exists in the ride-share industry,” Bird says.

 Bird believes that over the short-term, AVs won’t have much of an impact on drivers’ earnings. But over time, as AVs become more affordable and capable, they will compete for rides and represent a larger share of rides on the Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) platform. At this point, even with the “growing pie” drivers will start to feel the impact.

“That’s when we’ll know we’ve reached the next phase, when AVs join humans in a true hybrid network,” Bird says.

During this phase, Bird encourages drivers to differentiate themselves from AVs by handling baggage, assisting the elderly, and provide more interaction with riders.

Finally, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) wants to help drivers become AVs entrepreneurs. The company said it will help drive down the costs and barriers to entry to AV ownership. With relationships with autonomous tech providers, auto manufacturers, and financial institutions, Lyft can offer things like novel financing arrangements or “commonsense policy frameworks.”

“Much as Airbnb powered a generation of host-entrepreneurs, we hope to see Lyft’s AV platform sparking a new wave of driver-entrepreneurs.”

data.title: French yachtmaker Beneteau withholds profit forecast amid U.S. trade doubts
data.description:
Beneteau (OTCPK:BTEAF), a French yacht and powerboat manufacturer with approximately 20% of its revenue coming from the U.S. market, opted against issuing a yearly profit forecast because of ongoing uncertainty surrounding U.S. trade policies under the Trump administration.

The decision to withhold earnings guidance stems largely from unpredictable trade negotiations, which "may shift one way one day and another the next," Chief Executive Bruno Thivoyon said during a conference call on Thursday.

Tensions between the United States and the European Union continue to escalate after the Trump administration imposed 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports. In response, the EU announced countermeasures affecting as much as €26 billion of U.S. goods. Trump has also warned of imposing a 200% tariff on EU-produced wine, champagne,and other alcoholic beverages unless European officials rescind their retaliatory tariffs, most of which are expected to take effect by mid-April.

Thivoyon acknowledged that if significant tariffs are placed on small motorboats manufactured in Europe, Beneteau's existing U.S. operations could potentially mitigate the effect by adjusting production strategies.

Based in western France, Beneteau (OTCPK:BTEAF) has faced financial headwinds, with its 2024 operating income plunging by 60% as rising interest rates led boat dealers to scale back inventories. The company anticipates further revenue declines this year but expects business to recover in the latter half as inventory levels stabilize and new product lines are introduced.

Beneteau (OTCPK:BTEAF) proposed a dividend payout of €1.43 ($1.55) per share, which includes a special interim dividend of €1.21 per share following the sale of its housing division. This payment is scheduled for March 27.

data.title: Anthropic's Claude adds web search, joins growing list of AI search engines
data.description:
Amazon-backed (NASDAQ:AMZN) Anthropic, a generative artificial intelligence startup, added web search to its popular large language model, Claude, the company announced today.

"When Claude incorporates information from the web into its responses, it provides direct citations so you can easily fact check sources," Anthropic said. "Instead of finding search results yourself, Claude processes and delivers relevant sources in a conversational format. This enhancement expands Claude's extensive knowledge base with real-time insights, providing answers based on more current information."

Anthropic's Claude joins a growing list of AI-powered search engines, including Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) AI Overviews, Microsoft (MSFT) Bing with Copilot, OpenAI's ChatGPT Search and Perplexity AI.

Claude's new web search is available now in feature preview for all paid Claude users in the U.S. Anthropic expects to add the web search support for users on its free plan and in more countries soon.

San Francisco-based Anthropic reached a $61.5B valuation earlier this month following its latest funding round. It achieved annualized revenue of $1.4B this month as well.

data.title: Private equity exec and superfan Bill Chisholm buys the Boston Celtics for $6.1B
data.description:
Managing partner and investment chief at private equity firm Symphony Technology Group (STG), William Chisholm, along with a syndicate of investors, struck a $6.1B deal to buy the Boston Celtics basketball team, according to multiple media reports on Thursday.

A report from Bloomberg said Sixth Street (TSLX) committed more than $1B to the deal, and Chisholm’s consortium had emerged as the front-runner for the franchise.

Owners of the reigning NBA champions, Boston Basketball Partners, decided to sell the popular team last year and were seeking offers for a majority stake. 

The current owners, which include Wyc Grousbeck and Bain Capital’s (BCSF) Stephen Pagliuca, had bought the NBA team back in 2002 for $360M.

Reports suggested Pagliuca wanted the ownership of the Celtics but was outbid by Chisholm. He was “saddened to find out that we have not been selected in the process.”

The purchase price for the Celtics is the highest ever for a U.S. sports franchise, following the deal for the NFL's Washington Commanders for $6.05B in 2023, which was bought by a group led by Apollo Global (APO) cofounder Josh Harris and Danaher (DHR) cofounder Mitchell Rales.

According to STG, Chisholm is also the cofounder of the private equity firm, which has $10B of assets under management as of March 2023.

Chisholm is reportedly a die-hard Celtics fan and hails from Georgetown, Massachusetts, with an "encyclopedic knowledge" of the team. The team is the fourth-most valuable NBA franchise after the Warriors, the Knicks, and the Lakers.

data.title: ARS Pharmaceuticals outlines 2025 growth strategy with neffy commercialization and global expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ARS Pharmaceuticals (SPRY) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Richard Lowenthal, Co-Founder and CEO, highlighted the successful launch of neffy 2mg in the U.S. and the FDA approval for neffy 1mg for pediatric use, targeting children weighing 15-30 kilograms. He emphasized that this demographic represents 23% of the current auto-injector market.

  • Management stressed the anticipated expansion in payer coverage, with over 51% of commercially insured patients being able to access neffy without prior authorization by April 1. They project reaching 80% unrestricted coverage by summer 2025.

  • CEO Lowenthal discussed the U.S. epinephrine market opportunity, valued at $3 billion in net sales annually, with an additional $7 billion potential among untreated patients.

  • CFO Kathleen Scott reported $86.6 million total revenue for Q4 2024, including $6.7 million from neffy sales. She elaborated on the complexities of revenue recognition from the ALK licensing agreement, which contributed $73.5 million in collaboration revenue.

  • Eric Karas, Chief Commercial Officer, outlined the company's multi-pronged strategy to increase neffy adoption, focusing on education, payer coverage, and patient awareness. He noted that neffy generated $7.3 million in net product revenue in its initial quarter.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates a significant ramp in neffy prescriptions following increased payer coverage, especially with the addition of CVS Caremark and other major insurers by July 1, 2025.

  • The company expects the pediatric neffy 1mg dose to be available by May 2025, aligning with its strategy to capitalize on the back-to-school season.

  • Global expansion plans include commercial launches in Germany and the U.K. by summer 2025, with regulatory decisions in Canada, China, and Japan expected by year-end.

Financial Results

  • ARS Pharmaceuticals reported Q4 2024 revenue of $86.6 million, driven by $6.7 million in neffy sales and $73.5 million from the ALK agreement.

  • Full-year 2024 revenue totaled $89.1 million, with $7.3 million from neffy sales.

  • The company ended the year with $314 million in cash and equivalents, providing a three-year runway for its current operating plan.

  • SG&A expenses for Q4 2024 were $35.5 million, reflecting marketing and launch investments for neffy.

Q&A

  • Ryan Deschner from Raymond James asked about the ramp for neffy 1mg relative to the 2mg format. CEO Lowenthal stated that the pediatric 1mg dose could significantly impact sales, given its target demographic.

  • Alexa Deemer of Cantor Fitzgerald inquired about the epinephrine market opportunities beyond retail. Lowenthal noted ongoing discussions with kit manufacturers for airlines and institutions, anticipating growth in non-retail segments over time.

  • Louise Chen from Scotia Bank raised concerns about prior authorization renewals and the potential for over-the-counter epinephrine. Lowenthal explained the administrative burden of prior authorizations and downplayed the likelihood of OTC approval due to regulatory hurdles.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on expanding payer coverage and addressing prior authorization barriers.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, especially regarding payer engagement and the potential market impact of neffy 1mg.

  • The tone in Q&A exchanges reflected a mix of enthusiasm for growth opportunities and acknowledgment of challenges in scaling adoption.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 reflected a stronger focus on payer coverage and overcoming administrative barriers compared to Q3 2024.

  • The launch of neffy 1mg for pediatric use marked a significant advancement from the previous quarter's efforts.

  • Management's tone in Q4 was more assertive, driven by tangible progress in market adoption and global expansion plans.

Risks and Concerns

  • The need for prior authorizations remains a barrier to prescribing, limiting the immediate uptake of neffy.

  • The company faces challenges in converting untreated patients and expanding into non-retail markets like airlines and schools.

  • Potential regulatory hurdles for global expansion could delay market entry in key regions.

Final Takeaway

ARS Pharmaceuticals is poised for growth with the U.S. launch of neffy and its expansion into global markets. The company's focus on payer coverage, pediatric applications, and non-retail opportunities underpins its strategy to address the $3 billion U.S. epinephrine market while tapping into an additional $7 billion in potential sales. With a robust cash position and strategic investments, ARS is well-positioned to drive adoption and scale its operations in 2025 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Value or Growth? SA spotlights 10 stocks that offer both
data.description:
Value and growth - two important factors traders and investors look for when allocating their capital towards the market. With Wall Street stumbling in 2025, it may seem challenging to find names that offer both traits.

As a result, through the use of Seeking Alpha’s quant metrics, here is a curated list of stocks that offer investors both characteristics. These U.S. stocks have market caps of at least $1B and all have quant metric ratings of an A- to an A+ in both the areas of growth and value. 

The screened list is ranked by year-to-date performance:

No. 10: Genworth Financial (GNW), -1% YTD.

No. 9: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company (GT), +0.4% YTD.

No. 8: Goldman Sachs BDC (GSBD), +0.9% YTD.

No. 7: Sinclair (SBGI), +1.6% YTD.

No. 6: UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC), +2.4% YTD. 

No. 5: F&G Annuities & Life (FG), +3.1% YTD.

No. 4: Pilgrim's Pride Corporation (PPC), +11.2% YTD.

No. 3: Clearway Energy (CWEN), +14.1% YTD.

No. 2: Bridge Investment Group Holdings (BRDG), +19.1% YTD.

No. 1: Super Micro Computer (SMCI), +31.5% YTD.

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Value ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VTV), (NYSEARCA:VBR), (NYSEARCA:SPYV), (BATS:EFV), (NYSEARCA:AVUV), (NYSEARCA:IWS), (NYSEARCA:DFUV), (NYSEARCA:DFAT), (NYSEARCA:DFIV), and (NYSEARCA:IJJ). 

Growth ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VUG), (NYSEARCA:IWF), (NYSEARCA:IVW), (NYSEARCA:SCHG), (NYSEARCA:SPYG), (NASDAQ:VONG), (NYSEARCA:MGK), (NASDAQ:IUSG), and (NYSEARCA:VBK).

data.title: Hudbay sees interest in U.S. copper project from Middle East, Japan - Bloomberg
data.description:
Hudbay Minerals (NYSE:HBM) is in talks with investors from Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Japan to sell a minority stake in its flagship Copper World project in Arizona, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Hudbay (NYSE:HBM) CEO Peter Kukielski told Bloomberg that he met with "a lot of the key interested parties" in Saudi Arabia in November, and potential investors include Saudi's Manara Minerals Investment Co., as well as Japanese trading houses and investors from UAE.

The sale process is expected to conclude within six months, with Hudbay (HBM) planning to sell up to 30% of the project, according to the report.

Hudbay (HBM) expects the proposed 85K metric tons/year Copper World project will boost its total annual copper production by more than 50% from current levels; in January, the Arizona Department of Environmental Quality issued its air quality permit for the project, which marked the completion of the project's permitting process.
